_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.

current_orchestrator_session.primary_focus: Cosmos page Phase 4d arc (v0.208 → v0.212) — 5 minor releases. Operator's stated wishes (time controls, pill labels, real conjunctions/eclipses, Lagrange points, geostationary orbit) all shipped against the live cosmos page.

current_focus: 2026-05-12..15 mega-session continued: shipped v0.208→v0.242 (~35 minor releases). Cosmos Phase 4d arc (v0.208-v0.213: time controls, pill labels, conjunctions/eclipses, Lagrange, reference orbits, body_pill widget extraction). Then pill widget polish (v0.214-v0.219 per operator screenshots: panel-below-pill layout, layering, cap-as-body-color, 3-circles-same-size, planet-natural-size). Then a long chat/server-settings/roles arc: v0.222 remove dead channel hint, v0.223 chat polish (blank-line gap, Þ-only reaction popup, ts-expansion, removed Groups cog), v0.224 mod_action handler (Kick/Ban/Mod actually work), v0.225 block popup behind modals, v0.226-228 kick edge cases (registered_names cleanup + name-fallback for empty-key users), v0.229-230 stateful popup hover + animated Þ + sticky region, v0.231 alphabetical sort DMs/Groups/Servers, v0.232-234 clipboard image paste (finally fixed at the WINIT LAYER — egui-winit swallows Ctrl+V), v0.235-236 @mention autocomplete (Enter/click/arrows + caret-to-end + clickable message mentions), v0.237-238 upload-storage limits as server settings (total disk cap + per-rank FIFO), v0.239 ROLES PHASE R1 (data-driven roles table + capability gates + WS), v0.240 always-editable server policy + eye/federation channel icons, v0.241 ROLES R2 (assignment dropdown + data-driven badges), v0.242 ROLES R3 (Server Settings roles editor — family-streaming flow COMPLETE end-to-end). New design doc: docs/design/roles-system.md (R1-R4 phasing; R4 = consolidate per-role limits into roles table, deferred).  v0.243 Follow/Unfollow wire fix (target->target_key). v0.244 sidebar channel-status icons (eye=read-only, node-graph=federated, painted after # name in server+group lists). v0.245 FULL BAN MANAGEMENT (banned_keys name column + BannedUser/list_banned/ban_user/unban_user + BannedListRequest/BannedList/Unban WS + Server Settings Banned-users panel; modal Ban now truly persists + is reversible; fixed irreversible-ban trap from registered_names deletion). name-only-kick admin-protection security gap, IO/macro page (scoping deferred — operator dismissed), Cosmos 4d-quad/4b/4g/Map-Room, PQ chat migration, Debian 11->12. v0.246 MUTE REWORK (muted_members table orthogonal to roles; modal Mute now works; /mute /unmute no longer clobber role; legacy role=muted honored + cleaned on unmute; MOD-section Muted-users panel with per-row Unmute). v0.247 SECURITY: name-only kick/ban/mute now runs the same admin/owner-protection + self-guard as the key path (resolves all keys_for_name; widened protected set to admin OR owner). v0.248 COSMOS 4d-quad (eclipse shadow-axis sub-point lat/lon via obliquity+GMST; forward 400-day Sky-Events scan with cached/throttled recompute; 3 validation tests). PQ chat migration deliberately NOT done autonomously — flagged as needing operator involvement (foundational identity change). v0.249 ban/mute storage regression tests (9, lock v0.245-247 invariants incl. mute-not-clobber-role + irreversible-ban-trap). Verified Tier-3 infinite-of-x already done (journal was stale). PQ chat migration intentionally deferred to operator.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.249: added 9 storage regression tests (src/relay/storage/channels.rs ban_mute_tests) locking the v0.245/246/247 invariants — ban captures name (irreversible-ban-trap guard), set_banned back-compat, empty-key no-op, re-ban replaces not duplicates, MUTE-DOES-NOT-CLOBBER-ROLE (the core v0.246 fix), legacy role=muted cleared to unverified on unmute, unmute role-safe for non-muted, ban/mute independence. Followed the existing dms.rs fresh_db() Storage::open temp-file pattern. Also VERIFIED + corrected a stale journal gap: the 5 Tier-3 infinite-of-x items were already migrated in v0.133 (loaders+data+fetch all present); only calculator buttons remain and those are explicitly acceptable.
recent_decisions.rationale: Just shipped 3 moderation/security changes (v0.245-247) into msg_handlers/storage with ZERO automated coverage; the journal also lists msg_handlers test coverage as a pending audit item. Storage-layer tests are zero-risk, need no network harness, and pin the exact design decisions (esp. mute-orthogonal-to-role) so a future refactor can not silently reintroduce the role-clobber data-loss bug. Deliberately did NOT start the PQ chat migration autonomously — foundational identity/crypto change, operator-stated-direction-not-yet-shipped, high blast radius (auto-mode caution).

recent_decisions.decision: Cosmos Phase 4d-quad shipped (v0.248): (1) eclipse shadow-axis ground point — subpoint_lat_lon_deg() converts the Earth->Moon ecliptic vector to geographic lat/lon using J2000 mean obliquity (23.4393 deg physical constant) + the IAU GMST polynomial; both exact in the model J2000 time base. Honest approximation (sub-lunar point, ignores parallax/oblateness/dT) — labeled approx in the HUD. (2) Forward Sky-Events scan — scan_upcoming_sky_events() steps sim_time 3h for 400 days, detecting eclipses by enter/exit windows (refined to 1-min peak) and tight (<1deg) conjunctions/occultations by per-pair local-minimum-separation turning points (refined). Cached in GuiState cosmos_upcoming_* with a 12h-drift + 300ms-wall throttle so the ~3000-sample scan never runs per-frame. HUD: eclipse readout gains a shadow-axis-over-~LAT/LON line; Sky-events box gains an upcoming section (in Xd (date) label, severity color). Validated by 3 unit tests incl. sub-solar latitude must hit +/-23.44 over a year (pins obliquity sign+magnitude; celestial pole P=(0,cose,sine) derived for the model Y-up/XZ-ecliptic frame).
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item; continues the in-flight Cosmos 4d arc (4d-tri instant detector explicitly TODO-ed 4d-quad). Chose this over PQ chat migration: PQ is a foundational identity/security migration the operator stated as direction-not-yet-shipped — too high blast radius to do autonomously without operator involvement (auto-mode caution rule). 4d-quad is self-contained, math-validated, zero security surface.

recent_decisions.decision: Closed the name-only-kick admin-protection security gap (v0.247). handle_mod_action previously only ran the "non-admin cannot act on an admin" check when an explicit target KEY was supplied; the name-only path (target empty, target_name set) skipped it entirely — a rogue mod could kick/ban an admin by name. Fix: resolve candidate_keys = [target key if any] + keys_for_name(target_name) (dedup), then run BOTH protections against the whole set: (a) self-action guard now catches name-only self-target (and extended to mute), (b) admin/owner protection refuses a non-admin if ANY resolved registration is admin/owner. Also widened protected set from admin-only to admin OR owner.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list security item. The TODO had said "currently fine because only admins use moderation UI in practice" — that is a trust assumption, not a control. Mission = civilization-scale, billions of users, AI agents as mods; the control must hold without assuming who uses the UI. Self-mute now blocked too (footgun: a mod muting themselves has no easy modal self-recovery).

recent_decisions.decision: Mute reworked off the role bandaid (v0.246). The OLD /mute (broadcast.rs handle_mod_command) set user_roles.role=muted — clobbering the real role (donor/verified/mod) — and /unmute reset role=user (a non-existent role -> default-denies to unverified). Data loss: mute+unmute a Donor and they came back unverified. The modal Mute (mod_action mute) was outright unimplemented (returned not-implemented-use-kick). Built a muted_members table ORTHOGONAL to roles: is_muted/mute_user/unmute_user/list_muted + MutedUser struct (mirrors banned_keys/BannedUser). unmute_user also clears any LEGACY role=muted row, best-effort reset to unverified (their original role was already lost by the old bandaid). relay.rs Chat handler now gates on role==muted OR is_muted() (legacy honored). New WS: MutedListRequest/MutedList{target}/Unmute (mod-gated; MutedList targeted via send-loop filter, never leaks). mod_action mute|unmute implemented (multi-key via keys_for_name, notifies actor + target). Slash /mute /unmute now call mute_user/unmute_user (no role clobber). New MOD-section Server Settings > Muted users panel (per-row Unmute, mirrors Banned users). Mute stays mod-level; the existing admin-protection check (any action on an admin by non-admin is refused) already covers mute-an-admin.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item: muted_members table for Mute. Doing it as a separate table (not role=muted) is the no-bandaid choice — mute must not destroy role state. Mirrored the v0.245 ban architecture 1:1 for consistency (same panel pattern, same targeted-WS-delivery, same chrono-free date fmt).

recent_decisions.decision: Full ban management (v0.245). The modal Ban button (handle_mod_action) never persisted to banned_keys — Ban silently == Kick. Wired it: ban_user(key,name) inserts into banned_keys, force-closes live sockets (kicked_keys + peers.remove for BOTH kick and ban now), and the identify gate (relay.rs:2320, already existed) rejects banned keys on reconnect. CRITICAL discovery: the kick path deletes the user from registered_names, so /unban <name> (resolves via keys_for_name) could no longer find a key — a ban was IRREVERSIBLE. Fix per no-bandaids rule: banned_keys gained a name column (idempotent ALTER + fresh CREATE), new storage BannedUser struct + list_banned()/ban_user()/unban_user() (set_banned kept as name-less delegator for slash /ban /unban back-comat). New WS: BannedListRequest{} (admin-gated), BannedList{users,target:Option} (targeted to requesting admin via send-loop filter so the ban list never broadcasts to non-admins), Unban{target}. New Server Settings > ADMIN > Banned users panel: auto-requests once/session, Refresh button, per-row name/short-key/date + Unban. chat_banned_users + chat_banned_requested GuiState fields; chat_banned_requested reset on ws disconnect. Slash /ban now uses ban_user too. Past-tense verb fix ("baned"->"Banned").
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item "banned_keys wire-up so Ban prevents rejoin". A moderation feature that silently half-works is exactly the kind of bandaid the mission doc forbids. Built the full reversible loop (ban persists + unban UI) rather than just the insert, because a ban with no working unban is irreversible-by-accident.

recent_decisions.decision: Data-driven roles system (docs/design/roles-system.md). New `roles` SQLite table seeded with 5 built-ins (unverified/verified/donor/mod/admin); each role has can_stream/can_upload/can_voice capabilities + a base_tier that inherits the existing server_settings per-tier numeric limits (avoids a big limit migration; R4 consolidates later). Capability composition: effective_can_X = server_master_X_toggle && role.can_X. Assignment via the chat user-modal Role dropdown (set_user_role WS, generalizes mod_action mod/unmod). Built-in roles: caps editable, id/trust/base_tier locked, undeletable. Shipped R1 (v0.239 backend), R2 (v0.241 client parse + dropdown + data-driven badges), R3 (v0.242 Server Settings roles editor). R4 (move per-role limits into the roles table, deprecate base_tier) deferred until per-custom-role limits are actually wanted.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator wanted custom ranks + 'my lil bro big bro dad etc should be able to stream to our vps relay' WITHOUT promoting them to mod/admin. Chose (via AskUserQuestion) fully data-driven roles + per-role stream/upload/voice + role-dropdown assignment. Per infinite-of-x: roles are data not code. BEHAVIOR CHANGE shipped in v0.239: admin streaming now also requires the server-wide video_streaming_enabled master toggle (default OFF; old start path ignored it) — operator was told to flip it on in Server policy.

recent_decisions.decision: Clipboard image paste (Ctrl+V) MUST be detected at the raw winit layer (src/lib.rs window_event), NOT through egui's input. egui-winit intercepts the Cut/Copy/Paste shortcuts, reads clipboard TEXT only, and returns early WITHOUT emitting the key event — so for an image-only clipboard egui's input has zero signal. Took 3 attempts (v0.232 post-TextEdit, v0.233 top-of-draw — both read egui input, both failed; v0.234 winit-layer flag — works). The bug class is confined to exactly Cut/Copy/Paste; audited all of src/, no other instances. Future shortcut handling that competes with egui-winit's swallowed shortcuts must use the winit layer.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: print-screen then Ctrl+V worked on web but not the native app. The architectural lesson (egui-winit swallows C/X/V) is the durable takeaway — captured in lib.rs comments + this journal so it isn't rediscovered.

recent_decisions.decision: Split items toxicology: ingredients hold their own tox in sidecar RON files; items are pure ingredient lists with versioned `history` array. Toxicology derived at query time from the current ingredient list.
recent_decisions.rationale: User asked: 'wouldn't it be better to just list the item and ingredients and then from that list of ingredients determines toxicology' + 'we also want to make sure we accommodate the fact that the ingredients list might change for something like Oreos.' Both improvements landed in v0.116.1.

recent_decisions.decision: Multi-AI coordination = static registry (data/coordination/agent_registry.ron) + runtime claim table (agent_sessions SQLite) + audit JSON files (data/coordination/sessions/*.json) + aggregator (node scripts/agent-status.js).
recent_decisions.rationale: User wanted a way for fresh AI sessions to check if a scope already has work in progress, see completion estimates, and know if their assigned scope is at a stop state.

recent_decisions.decision: Going forward, ALWAYS git-add untracked .rs/.ron/.csv before pushing. Saved as feedback_untracked_files.md.
recent_decisions.rationale: v0.108 created issuer_trust.rs but never staged it. CI broke 8 releases later when desktop-build CI tried fresh checkout.

Audit found federation.rs:220 + federation.rs:461 had open TODOs for client signature verification. Forward-compatible verifier landed without breaking current unsigned-client federation. Closes Tier 1.1 of the audit punch list.

recent_decisions.decision: Doc sweep landed in v0.122.0: SOP.md/STATUS.md/CONTRIBUTING.md/SYNC.md/SELF-HOSTING.md/ONBOARDING.md/Justfile/4 design docs all rewritten to drop dead `server/`, `crates/`, `native/`, `app/` paths and the dead `humanity-relay` package name.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 2 — these docs were misleading future contributors and AI agents who'd grep for paths that no longer exist. CLAUDE.md/README/FEATURES were already correct; the rest had drifted. STATUS.md flipped 16 scaffolded systems + electrical + psychology from ✅ to ⚠️ — the 'truthfulness rule' is now stated explicitly in the header.

recent_decisions.decision: `just theme` recipe added; runs `node scripts/gen-theme-css.js` to regenerate `web/shared/theme.css` from `data/gui/theme.ron`. Wired into `just ship` so push-time always regenerates. Idempotent.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 5 — script existed but wasn't wired into any build step, so theme.css drift was inevitable. Closes that gap.

recent_decisions.decision: Audit Session 2 (Tier 3) landed in v0.123.0. Seven hardcoded arrays migrated to data files: `data/inventory/equipment_slots.json`, `data/bugs/taxonomy.json`, `data/crafting/categories.json`, `data/market/categories.json`, `data/resources/catalog.json`, `data/studio/{scenes,sources}.json`, `data/themes/presets.json`. New `pub fn load_*` helpers in `src/gui/mod.rs` populate `GuiState` at startup; pages read from `state.X`. JS theme presets regenerated by `scripts/gen-theme-presets-js.js`, wired into `just theme`.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 3 (Infinite-of-X) — design rule says 'anything that can exist more than once is a data file, not code'. The migrations close the 8 hardcoded violations the audit found in `docs/design/infinite-of-x.md`. Pattern: top-level `load_*(data_dir)` returning owned Vec, graceful fallback to empty Vec on missing/malformed input — pages render an empty filter row rather than crashing. Type-renamed legacy `Resource`/`ResourceCategory` (resources.rs-private, `&'static str`) into owned `ResourceEntry`/`ResourceCategory` in `gui/mod.rs`. Studio types kept; new `StudioScenePreset`/`StudioSourcePreset` deserialise from JSON and convert to `StudioScene`/`StudioSource` via `studio_scene_from_preset`/`studio_source_from_preset`.

recent_decisions.decision: BUG-034 fixed in v0.124.0. `.github/workflows/build-desktop.yml` now publishes raw binaries (`HumanityOS-<platform>.exe` for Windows, plain executable for Linux/macOS) alongside the existing `.tar.gz` bundles. `src/updater.rs::find_platform_asset` prefers raw binary assets and refuses archive-only releases (returns 'No binary for this platform') instead of silently writing gzip bytes as a corrupt exe.
recent_decisions.rationale: User reported in-app updater on Windows produced a binary that failed to launch with 'Unsupported 16-Bit Application'. Investigation: corrupted file was 38612146 bytes — exactly the size of `HumanityOS-windows-x64.tar.gz`. The updater downloaded the only release asset (a tar.gz bundle) and renamed it to .exe without extracting. `file` on the corrupted exe confirms gzip magic bytes. Two-part fix: workflow ships both formats; updater prefers raw.

recent_decisions.decision: Audit Session 3 (Tier 1.2 + Tier 4) lands alongside the BUG-034 fix in v0.124.0. Storage tests added: 7 for `dms.rs` (DM persistence, E2EE ciphertext preservation, direction-symmetric load, conversation isolation, mark-read, ECDH requires registered name, limit returns recent N) and 10 for `trading.rs` (escrow happy-path, complete requires both confirms, complete idempotent, item changes reset confirmations, cancelled cannot complete, third-party cannot confirm, cannot confirm pending, rejected→cancelled, unknown side rejected, list filters by party). Total lib tests: 173 → 190. Tier 4 dual-UI parity table added to `docs/design/ui-system.md`.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 1.2 — msg_handlers.rs et al had 0 tests over 3081 LOC. Storage-layer tests are the right scope (handler tests would need a heavy RelayState fixture). The new tests cover the load-bearing escrow correctness invariants the audit flagged.

recent_decisions.decision: BUG-035 + BUG-036 fixed in v0.125.0. (035) Native chat reply disappears after WS blip — fixed by no-op'ing channel-switch when the click target equals the active channel (chat.rs, 5 sites) and adding chat_sent_timestamps dedup to the HTTP history fetch (lib.rs). (036) Deleted system channels resurrect on every relay restart — fixed by gating the seed list with a `default_channels_seeded` row in server_state, with a one-shot migration that respects existing operators' channel sets when message_count > 0.
recent_decisions.rationale: User reported a reply going missing in #general after a connection blip, plus channel-deletes via the cog modal coming back. Investigation: chat_messages.clear() ran on every click (even same-channel re-clicks); the HTTP history fetch had no dedup; src/relay/mod.rs re-seeded 6 system channels on every restart and INSERT OR IGNORE happily re-inserted previously-deleted rows. Both bugs documented in docs/BUGS.md as BUG-035 and BUG-036 with full root-cause traces.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.126.0 reduces the default channel seed to `general` + `announcements` only. Welcome/Rules content snapshotted into `data/server/legacy_channel_content.json` for the future /welcome and /rules page migration. The `stream` and `dev` channels are dropped outright. Live VPS legacy channels (welcome/rules/stream/dev) deleted via SQL.
recent_decisions.rationale: User feedback: 'welcome and rules channels should be handled by HOS, not channels. Like proper pages dedicated to this stuff that we can easily update'. Channels are the wrong shape for static reference content; pages backed by data files are correct. v0.127.0 will build the actual /welcome /rules /accord pages.

recent_decisions.decision: Distribution-sovereignty plan documented in `docs/distribution-mirrors.md`. Recommended adoption order: (1) self-hosted Forgejo on VPS, (2) Codeberg mirror, (3) VPS release-binary mirror, (4) self-hosted BitTorrent tracker + seeder for data/asset payload, (5) Internet Archive seeder, (6) Software Heritage archival, (7) WinGet manifest. Steps 8+ scope-driven (IPFS, F-Droid/Flathub/Snap, Homebrew, Radicle, paid IPFS pinning).
recent_decisions.rationale: User wants HOS independent of GitHub, prefers self-hosting + torrent/magnet links to fit the P2P federation ethos. Vision: small exe is the entry point; on first run it pulls data/+assets/ from the torrent network with VPS as always-on seeder; client devices become seeders too. The doc is curated — only platforms that are good fits are listed. GitLab, SourceForge, Bitbucket, NotABug, Chocolatey were considered and dropped.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 1 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.127.0: self-hosted Forgejo at https://git.united-humanity.us/shaostoul/humanity. Forgejo v15.0.0 binary at /usr/local/bin/forgejo; user `forgejo`; data at /var/lib/forgejo; SQLite3; nginx reverse proxy 127.0.0.1:3000; Let's Encrypt TLS auto-renewing; self-registration disabled. Justfile `_commit` recipe now pushes to BOTH `origin` (GitHub) and `forge` (Forgejo); forge push is best-effort (`-` prefix in just) so a transient Forgejo outage doesn't block ship. Tags pushed to both remotes too (GitHub Actions Build Desktop App still keys off origin tag). Initial mirror: 12386 objects, 50 tags (v0.1.0 → v0.126.0), 2 branches (main + claude/happy-panini), 51 MB. `docs/forgejo-setup.md` documents the install + reproduce steps.
recent_decisions.rationale: Sovereignty layer is the keystone of the distribution-mirrors plan. Once forge holds the source, subsequent steps (release-binary mirror, BitTorrent seeder, Internet Archive backup, etc.) build on it. Two issues hit during install: Debian 11's git 2.30.2 too old for Forgejo (needed ≥2.34.1) — fixed by building 2.45.2 from source at /usr/local/bin/git; app.ini owned root:forgejo prevented the installer from saving config — fixed by chown forgejo:forgejo. Both documented in forgejo-setup.md so future operators don't repeat them.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 3 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.128.0: self-hosted release-binary mirror at https://united-humanity.us/releases/. nginx /releases/ location with autoindex; backfilled v0.122.0–v0.127.0; build-desktop.yml CI scp's binaries to VPS on every tag push via appleboy/scp-action; mirror-manifest job regenerates manifest.json + latest symlink via /usr/local/bin/regen-releases-manifest. Step 2 (Codeberg) skipped temporarily — operator awaiting signup confirmation email; will land in a follow-up release.
recent_decisions.rationale: The auto-updater (BUG-034 fix in v0.124.0) will gain this as a fallback URL later — for now the mirror is a working independent download path, anyone can wget from it without GitHub. The manifest at /releases/manifest.json + the latest symlink mean operators don't need to know specific version numbers; clients can fetch latest deterministically. nginx sites-enabled/humanity was a regular file (not a symlink to sites-available) — edits had to go directly to sites-enabled. Documented inline in the orchestrator notes.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.128.2 shipped a CI fix: per-matrix mirror step replaced with a single Linux mirror-to-vps job using actions/download-artifact + appleboy/scp-action. v0.128.3 followed with continue-on-error on the upload step + if: always() on the regen step, because scp-action sometimes returns a false-positive i/o timeout AFTER the upload completes successfully. Workflow is now self-healing.
recent_decisions.rationale: Original v0.128.0 workflow used appleboy/scp-action per-matrix-job, but Docker container actions only run on Linux runners — macOS/Windows runners failed with 'Container action is only supported on Linux'. v0.128.2 consolidated the upload into one Linux job. v0.128.3 added tolerance for the action's tendency to return false-positive timeouts after successful uploads. Both fixes verified end-to-end in CI.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 4 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.129.0: BitTorrent seeder + magnet URIs. transmission-daemon installed on VPS (RPC localhost-only, peer port 51413/tcp+udp). /usr/local/bin/torrent-create-and-seed script creates .torrent files with 5 public trackers + WebSeed pointing back at the HTTPS mirror, then adds them to transmission. /usr/local/bin/regen-releases-manifest extended to a two-phase script: (1) seed any unseeded .tar.gz, (2) build manifest with magnet URIs. Backfilled 36 torrents covering v0.122.0–v0.128.3 (~1.4 GB seeding). docs/torrent-infrastructure.md documents the full setup.
recent_decisions.rationale: Matches the user's original P2P architecture vision: VPS as always-on seed; client devices become seeders too via swarm participation. WebSeed makes the critical path tracker-independent — even with zero peers and every public tracker down, BitTorrent clients fall back to plain HTTPS from the VPS. CI required NO workflow change because the regen script now handles both seeding and manifest generation. Self-hosted tracker (opentracker) deferred — not blocking because of WebSeed.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 4.5 landed in v0.130.0: layered packages + file-level manifest. New scripts/gen-data-manifest.js walks data/ + assets/icons/ + assets/shaders/, writes data-manifest-<tag>.json with per-file SHA-256 hash + Forgejo per-file URL (https://git.united-humanity.us/shaostoul/humanity/raw/tag/<tag>/<path>). bundle-data CI job now generates the manifest + uploads it as a release asset + as a workflow artifact. mirror-to-vps job depends on bundle-data so it picks up the data archive + manifest via download-artifact. Backfilled 10 data archives + 10 data manifests for v0.122.0–v0.129.0. transmission auto-seeded 10 new data-archive torrents (50 total now, ~3.6 GB).
recent_decisions.rationale: User instinct: 'shipping 38 MB for a 4 KB recipe edit is wasteful, and we should be able to inspect what's changing'. The layered architecture (binary / data / assets as separate torrents) plus file-level manifest enables future delta sync: a 4 KB data tweak becomes a 4 KB update. Forgejo's raw-file URLs serve as the per-file CDN — no IPFS or extra storage layer required. SHA-256 chosen over BLAKE3 to avoid extra deps in the manifest generator (Node stdlib only). Client-side delta sync UI (Step 4.6+) will read this manifest, compare to local files, and fetch only what changed.

recent_decisions.decision: Gameplay loop arc shipped v0.159 → v0.172 (14 minor releases). Closes Tier 1A (game systems stub completion) + Tier 1B (chat parity) and adds a 3-quest starter gameplay loop on top. AI agents now experience the loop end-to-end against the live VPS in 8 seconds: connect → identify → join → welcome (with quest + rooms) → perceive → talk to NPC → tour rooms → explore_ship completed (+100 XP) → meet_the_crew unlocked → talk to all 6 crew → meet_the_crew completed (+200 XP, 300 total) → survey_storage unlocked. World ticks 24/7, 6 NPCs per room with dialog/greetings/wander, ambient chatter every 45s. PERSIST_KEY bumped 8 times (v1 → v8). 196 tests passing. New pages: Testing (QA loop), Browser (curated bookmarks). New nav: two-tier preview (Reality/Sim/Tools/Settings), opt-in via [▤], persists.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator was AFK and asked Claude to 'keep developing'. Each release ships independently testable changes with QA tasks added to data/testing/qa_tasks.json (now 25 entries). Quest framework is data-driven (chain_next_quest matches on quest id, easily extensible). Live VPS validation each release confirmed real-world behavior. Bot demo shows complete gameplay loop in 8 seconds — operators see the system working end-to-end without manual play.

recent_decisions.decision: Cosmos Phase 4d arc shipped v0.208 → v0.212 (5 minor releases). Closes all of the operator's explicitly-stated requests from 2026-05-12: time controls (play/pause/scrub/Now/speed presets/reverse + Howard Hinnant date conversion, no chrono dep), pill-style body labels with collision-dodging + expand-to-card, live conjunction + solar eclipse detection from Earth's POV with visual highlights + HUD readout, Lagrange-point overlay (L1-L5 for Sun-Earth/Earth-Moon/Sun-Mars/Sun-Jupiter/Sun-Saturn with notable-parking tooltips JWST/Trojans/Queqiao etc.), reference-orbit rings (LEO/MEO/GEO/HEO + Lunar TLI for Earth + areostationary/Jovo-stationary/NRHO for Mars/Jupiter/Moon). New GuiState fields: cosmos_sim_time_seconds + cosmos_sim_speed + cosmos_last_real_instant + cosmos_sim_time_initialized + cosmos_expanded_body + cosmos_show_lagrange + cosmos_show_reference_orbits. New helpers: compute_lagrange_points (cube-root-mass-ratio for L1/L2/L3 + Rodrigues for L4/L5) + detect_sky_events (pairwise angular separation O(11²)≈55 pairs per frame from Earth POV) + reference_orbits_for (const &[ReferenceOrbit] per body) + draw_reference_orbits (96-segment polylines) + draw_body_info_card (auto-positioning + auto-flip pill expand cards). Cosmos doc §17a-sexies updated to be a complete shipping log + roadmap. cosmos.rs grew from ~1500 to ~2100 lines.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator quoted: 'as close to real as possible' + 'could we add a way to watch the orbits play fast forward, rewind, and skip/seek along days/weeks/years/etc' + 'all the planets line up in real life as they are today on May 12, 2026 then we could see what it'd look like on a great conjunction' + 'experience solar eclipses on the Earth' + 'pill UI for body labels — planet sprite on left, name on right, click expands to info card' + (earlier) 'include stuff like lagrange points and ... geostationary orbit'. All explicitly named features shipped. Three remaining Phase 4 items deferred with rationale in cosmos doc: 4d-quad (umbra lat/lon + forward scan), 4b (sprite→mesh wgpu integration), 4g (FPS AR-glasses overlay, requires player-in-world).

Universal Button widget — built v0.113.0 + v0.114.0. Use widgets::Button, don't roll your own egui::Button.

Federation profile-signature verifier — landed in v0.122.0. Don't re-add the TODO; instead implement client-side signing when ready, then flip should_accept_profile_gossip to reject empty signatures.
Doc drift in SOP/STATUS/CONTRIBUTING/SYNC/SELF-HOSTING/ONBOARDING + 4 design docs — fixed in v0.122.0. New drift can only come from new content.

Tier 3 Infinite-of-X for 7 hardcoded arrays (inventory slots, bugs taxonomy, crafting/market categories, resources catalog, studio scenes/sources, JS theme variants) — migrated in v0.123.0. Don't re-hardcode; edit the JSON files under `data/<scope>/`.

BUG-034 (updater corrupted binary by writing tar.gz as .exe) — fixed in v0.124.0. Workflow ships both raw binaries and tar.gz bundles; updater prefers raw. The fix is forward-only: pre-v0.124.0 releases only have .tar.gz, and the updater will refuse them with 'No binary for this platform' rather than corrupting the install. Don't add a tar.gz extraction fallback — rejecting is the correct behaviour given how the workflow is now structured.

how_to_resume: 2. Run `git status` and `git log --oneline -10` to see recent state.
3. Read this file (orchestrator_state.json) — the running journal.
4. Run `node scripts/agent-status.js` for per-scope coordinator-friendly summary.
5. Read docs/STATUS.md and docs/FEATURES.md for the static feature inventory.
6. Check data/coordination/agent_registry.ron for the canonical scope list.
